content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - claim hot-path:claim:<0> needed_for; In the v3 memory data model, Source is needed for re-check and explainability.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.1 `Source`
- note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; Source as immutable evidence layer; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Source` is the raw knowledge record for chat turns, tool outputs, imported notes, and external records; it serves as the primary evidence layer for re-check and explainability. Its design invariants require that a source not be silently changed, `observed_at` be stored separately from ingestion time, `content_hash` support dedupe, and delete policy be explicit.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the Source component role, fields, and invariants.
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, Profile is not the source of truth.; scope=Applies to Profile in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, a Claim must make sense without the original chat.; scope=Applies to Claim invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, controversial facts should be left in Claim or Note rather than pulled into Profile.; scope=Applies to Profile invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, explicit facts should be preferred over inference in Profile.; scope=Applies to Profile invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, explicit statements should be preferred over inference for Claims.; scope=Applies to Claim invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, if meaning is still too soft, Note should be preferred over a bad Claim.; scope=Applies to Claim invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, one Claim must equal one predicate application.; scope=Applies to Claim invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, Profile must be rebuildable.; scope=Applies to Profile invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, Profile must be short.; scope=Applies to Profile invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, relative time in Claims must be normalized.; scope=Applies to Claim invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<11> answers_question; In the v3 memory data model, retrieval_state answers how often an item should participate in search and whether it is in hot, warm, or cold memory, archived, or hidden from ordinary retrieval.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 2.2 retrieval-state semantics
- claim hot-path:claim:<12> must_not_be_mixed_with; In the v3 memory data model, truth status and retrieval_state are different axes and must not be mixed.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 2.2 truth-status and retrieval-state semantics
- claim hot-path:claim:<13> answers_question; In the v3 memory data model, truth status answers whether knowledge is current, superseded, refuted, or past the end of its temporal window.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 2.2 truth-status semantics
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Note as intermediate semantic memory fragment; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment for important, retrieval-useful context that should not yet be cemented as a hard fact; typical uses include design direction, decision rationale, evolving plans, hypotheses, lessons, doc digests, and contextual framing.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining Note purpose and use cases.
- claim hot-path:claim:<14> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, Source must not be silently changed.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.1 `Source`
- claim hot-path:claim:<15> has_model_layer_component; In the v3 memory data model, Source is the evidence layer component.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 1 model layer decomposition
- claim hot-path:claim:<16> needed_for; In the v3 memory data model, Claim is a key table for explainable retrieval.; scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<17> needed_for; In the v3 memory data model, scope_json is needed for retrieval and reconciliation.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 2.1 field purpose

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=MemoryRun; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=MemoryRun; reason=Target section explicitly discusses the MemoryRun data-model concept, matching the existing entity.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

### 3.9 `MemoryRun`

Audit trail и debug слой.

Нужен, чтобы видеть:
- какой prompt сработал
- на какой модели
- что именно было retrieved
- какой budget был выделен
- какие ops были предложены
- какие ops реально применились
- где pipeline сломался

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `run_id`
- `namespace_id`
- `run_type: route | retrieve_update | canonicalize | construct_notes | reconcile_notes | extract_claims | reconcile_claims | update_profile | update_tasks | consolidate_notes | repair_memory | apply_retention | read_plan | compose_answer | compact`
- `trigger_mode: hot_path | background | manual | slow_path`
- `source_ids_json`
- `retrieved_item_ids_json`
- `retrieval_budget_json`
- `prompt_name`
- `prompt_version`
- `model_name`
- `input_hash`
- `output_json`
- `applied_ops_json`
- `repair_actions_json`
- `latency_ms`
- `token_input`
- `token_output`
- `status: success | failed | partial`
- `error_text`
- `created_at`

Инварианты:
- run log не должен исчезать silently
- `input_hash` полезен для идемпотентности и retry safety

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"notes":[{"candidate_claim_hints":["MemoryRun is the audit trail and debug layer in the v3 memory data model.","MemoryRun records prompt, model, retrieved data, budget, proposed ops, applied ops, and pipeline failure points.","MemoryRun input_hash is useful for idempotency and retry safety."],"confidence":0.98,"entity_refs":[{"entity_id":"entity:<0>","role":"primary"}],"evidence_kind":"direct","evidence_quote":"Audit trail и debug слой.","evidence_reason":"The section explicitly defines `MemoryRun` as an audit and debug layer and then lists its purpose, fields, and invariants.","importance":4,"keywords":["MemoryRun","audit trail","debug layer","run_type","trigger_mode","input_hash","applied_ops_json","repair_actions_json"],"note_type":"doc_digest","rationale":"This is a reusable component digest covering role, workflow/debug context, field categories, and invariants; it is richer than a single atomic claim.","scope_json":{"basis":"summarized","kind":"document","text":"Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the MemoryRun component role, debug purpose, recommended fields, and invariants."},"summary":"In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is the audit trail and debug layer for the memory pipeline, recording which prompt and model ran, what was retrieved, what budget was allocated, which ops were proposed and applied, and where the pipeline broke. Recommended fields include identifiers, run/trigger type, source and retrieved item IDs, retrieval budget, prompt/model metadata, input/output hashes or JSON, applied/repair ops, latency, token counts, status, error text, and creation time.","tags":["doc_digest","data_model","memory_pipeline","debugging"],"title":"MemoryRun as audit trail and debug layer","valid_from":null,"valid_to":null}]}
